f1_composite_purpose: own#2 (b) multi-substrate convergence verdict (Putnam multi-realizability empirical anchor)

substrate_distinctness.scale: 0 (identical to existing N-tracks) to 1 (fully novel ontology)
substrate_distinctness.rationale: Photonic substrate is ontologically distinct from CLM (digital float), AKIDA (spike-event), EEG (biological signal), QRNG (quantum noise) -- continuous-amplitude optical field is a genuinely new substrate class

measurement_maturity.rationale: Zero published refs for IIT-Phi on photonic substrates (verified 2026-05-01 web search). Most immature among all N-tracks

access_path_readiness.rationale: All 4 vendors lack public cloud API. Vendor partnership or hardware purchase required. Pricing undisclosed for PCIe SKUs

falsifier_strength.scale: 0 (no falsifier) to 1 (full BIDIRECTIONAL with null + cross-axis)
falsifier_strength.rationale: 5 pre-registered predicates with null floor (PRED-4) + cross-axis dependency (PRED-2 with N-3, PRED-5 with N-4) + inter-vendor consistency (PRED-3)

N_19_pci_clinical.factors_estimate: distinctness 0.90, maturity 0.95 (PCI clinical gold standard), access 0.50 (TMS rental needed), falsifier 0.90

n13_contribution_scenarios.timepoint: T+6mo (estimated, vendor partnership + first P-A measurement)
n13_contribution_scenarios.f1_contribution: +0.5 to +1.0 percent of composite (marginal but real, adds 4th substrate axis)

n13_contribution_scenarios.timepoint: T+12mo (continuous-state IIT methodology if academic gap closes + 3-vendor cross via PRED-3)
n13_contribution_scenarios.f1_contribution: Substrate diversity 4th axis -- own#2 (b) 'Phi extends to optical substrate' strong closure

n13_role_in_f1: BOOST after majority of N-1 / N-3 / N-19 / N-11 / N-12 PASS. Acts as photonic substrate diversity anchor, not standalone closure mechanism
